question_text: A ball is dropped from a height of 10 metres. What happens to its energy as it falls?

options.label: A
options.text: Gravitational potential energy decreases and kinetic energy increases

options.label: B
options.text: Gravitational potential energy increases and kinetic energy decreases

options.label: C
options.text: Both gravitational potential energy and kinetic energy decrease

options.label: D
options.text: Both gravitational potential energy and kinetic energy stay the same

correct_answer: Gravitational potential energy decreases and kinetic energy increases
explanation: When a ball falls, it loses height so its gravitational potential energy decreases. As it falls, it speeds up so its kinetic energy increases. The total mechanical energy stays constant because the gravitational potential energy lost is converted into kinetic energy gained (ignoring air resistance).
